classic: Never have I ever ghosted someone after a first date.
Never have I ever lied about my age to get into a club or movie.
Never have I ever sent a risky text to the wrong person.
Never have I ever snooped through someone else's phone.
Never have I ever stolen something small from a hotel room.
Never have I ever skipped work or school just because I didn't want to go.
Never have I ever kissed more than one person in the same night.
Never have I ever accidentally said 'I love you' and immediately regretted it.
Never have I ever been thrown out of a bar or restaurant.
Never have I ever created a fake social media account to stalk someone.
guilty_pleasures: Never have I ever binge-watched an entire season of a show in one day.
Never have I ever eaten food that fell on the floor after the 5-second rule.
Never have I ever listened to a cheesy pop song on repeat for hours.
Never have I ever pretended to be on a phone call to avoid someone.
Never have I ever worn the same underwear two days in a row.
Never have I ever eaten a whole tub of ice cream by myself.
Never have I ever practiced an argument in the mirror.
Never have I ever bought something online and immediately regretted it.
Never have I ever stalked an ex's new partner on Instagram.
Never have I ever lied about seeing a critically acclaimed movie just to fit in.
rehaan: Never have I ever been completely overwhelmed by how loud this family gets when we are all together.
Never have I ever forgotten a cousin's name because there are just way too many of us.
Never have I ever been treated like a little kid just because I'm the youngest in the entire generation.
Never have I ever used my 'London accent' to get out of trouble or impress someone.
Never have I ever secretly missed the quietness of London while surrounded by the family chaos.
Never have I ever pretended to understand an inside joke that the rest of the family has been laughing about for years.
Never have I ever packed way too many winter clothes for a trip here, forgetting it's not London weather.
Never have I ever been shocked by how much food this family expects me to eat in one sitting.
Never have I ever had an aunt or uncle pinch my cheeks like a baby even though I'm growing up.
Never have I ever complained about the Wi-Fi speed compared to what I have in London.
Never have I ever felt like a celebrity just because I only visit every couple of years.
Never have I ever had to explain British slang to confused relatives in their 50s.
Never have I ever been asked 'so when are you moving back here?' by at least three different relatives today.
Never have I ever been dragged to a random relative's house to be 'shown off' since I'm back from the UK.
Never have I ever secretly wished I could join in on the daily family gossip I miss out on.
Never have I ever been totally confused by a family tradition that everyone else thinks is completely normal.
Never have I ever packed an entire suitcase full of snacks just to bring back to London.
Never have I ever been teased for being the 'baby's baby' since my mom is the youngest cousin.
Never have I ever tried to drink tea the 'British way' and been mocked by the uncles.
Never have I ever realized how lucky I am to have such a massive, crazy family wait for me.
rehaan_asks: Never have I ever gotten into a massive argument over a game of cards at a family party.
Never have I ever snuck out of my parents' house when I was a teenager.
Never have I ever lied to my parents about how much money I spent on shopping.
Never have I ever secretly thrown away food that my sibling or cousin cooked because it tasted awful.
Never have I ever used a fake British accent to try and sound fancy or impress someone.
Never have I ever been completely clueless about a new piece of technology but pretended I knew how to use it.
Never have I ever blamed a broken household item on one of the kids (or on me, Rehaan!).
Never have I ever purposely given wrong directions to a relative because I didn't want them visiting yet.
Never have I ever gossiped about someone who was literally sitting in the exact same room as me.
Never have I ever hidden my favorite snacks so that none of the other cousins or siblings could eat them.
Never have I ever been absolutely terrified of my parents when I brought home a bad report card.
Never have I ever faked being sick to skip school or a boring family event.
Never have I ever lied about how much a piece of jewelry or clothing cost so I wouldn't get lectured.
Never have I ever secretly laughed when one of the other cousins was getting scolded.
Never have I ever totally forgotten an anniversary or birthday and had to invent a lie on the spot.
Never have I ever dropped food on the floor and quickly served it to a guest anyway.
Never have I ever broken a traffic rule and tried to sweet-talk my way out of a ticket.
Never have I ever secretly thought I was the favorite child or favorite cousin.
Never have I ever accidentally insulted a high-ranking family member's cooking and caused a total scandal.
Never have I ever been caught checking my phone at the dinner table after yelling at the kids for doing the exact same thing.
bbf: Never have I ever used a family secret as blackmail material against a cousin.
Never have I ever shown up unannounced at another relative's house just to eat their food.
Never have I ever been the main topic of gossip on the family WhatsApp group.
Never have I ever completely exaggerated a story to make my side of the family look better.
Never have I ever stolen the spotlight at a family wedding or major event.
Never have I ever actively avoided sitting next to that one specific uncle who talks too much about politics.
Never have I ever 'borrowed' an expensive outfit or jewelry from a cousin and conveniently forgotten to return it.
Never have I ever accidentally forwarded a message to the entire family group that was meant for just one person.
Never have I ever judged someone else's parenting skills at a family gathering.
Never have I ever sneakily regifted a present to another family member.
Never have I ever taken credit for a dish I bought from a restaurant and claimed I cooked it.
Never have I ever lied about being 'stuck in traffic' when I was actually just running late.
Never have I ever faked an illness to get out of a tedious family pooja or dinner.
Never have I ever muted the chaotic family group chat for an entire year.
Never have I ever held a grudge over something that happened at a family vacation two decades ago.
Never have I ever tried to mediate a fight between the older siblings and ended up getting yelled at by both.
Never have I ever sneakily eaten the best part of the dessert before serving it to the guests.
Never have I ever covered up for a cousin when they were doing something totally forbidden in their teens.
Never have I ever compared my spouse to someone else's spouse in the family.
Never have I ever deeply appreciated how we all show up for each other, no matter what.
agra: Never have I ever pretended to know a random historical fact about the Taj Mahal just to sound smart.
Never have I ever complained about the Indian summer heat while eating a cold dessert.
Never have I ever forced Rehaan to try a spicy Indian dish just to see his reaction.
Never have I ever gotten completely lost walking back to my room at the ITC Mughal.
Never have I ever hyped up the city of Agra so much that it felt like an advertisement.
Never have I ever bought an overpriced Taj Mahal souvenir globe.
Never have I ever embarrassed the family in front of foreigners at a monument.
Never have I ever used 'Back when we lived in Agra...' as an excuse to win an argument.
Never have I ever taken a cliché photo holding the tip of the Taj Mahal.
Never have I ever stolen the fancy toiletries from an ITC hotel room.
Never have I ever dramatically over-explained Mughal history to someone who wasn't listening.
Never have I ever insisted the food in London doesn't even compare to authentic Agra street food.
Never have I ever snuck an extra piece of Petha when the grandmas weren't looking.
Never have I ever made Rehaan translate British slang for the grandmas.
Never have I ever felt incredibly lucky to be back in this city with four generations of the family.
